title: celtis malagasica
intro: Celtis malagasica, also known as the Malagasy hackberry, is a plant species native to Madagascar, belonging to the family Cannabaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Celtis malagasica include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, while also acting as an antiseptic. The herbal preparations of Celtis malagasica can be found in various forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
